title: padus cornuta var. villosa
intro: Padus cornuta var. villosa, also known as the flowering dogwood or cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Padus cornuta var. villosa include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
